cleaned_narrative:
The candidate is a highly experienced Software Engineer with over 8 years of experience, primarily focused on C++ development for system software, medical devices, and gaming platforms. Their expertise spans C++ (including modern standards), Python, multithreading, HLD/LLD, and general embedded systems. They have demonstrated leadership capabilities, having led design and development efforts and managed teams.

However, this candidate is not a good fit for the Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. The job description is highly specialized, requiring deep expertise in the automotive domain, specifically with AUTOSAR architecture, ISO 26262 functional safety, Automotive SPICE processes, and specific automotive microcontrollers like Infineon Aurix. The candidate's profile lacks any mention of automotive industry experience, these critical frameworks, safety standards, or communication protocols like CAN/CAN-FD. While they possess strong general software engineering skills, the absence of domain-specific mandatory requirements makes them unsuitable for this particular role.

Recommendation: REVIEW, as the core automotive embedded requirements are not met, despite strong general software engineering experience.
key_strengths: Extensive Software Development Experience:** Over 8 years of experience in designing and developing robust software systems across various industries.
Strong C++ Proficiency:** Expert-level proficiency in C++ (11/14/17) and STL, along with experience in multithreading and system-level programming.
Leadership and Mentorship Potential:** Demonstrated experience in leading design and development efforts and leading teams of developers, providing guidance and direction.
Architectural Design Skills:** Experience with High-Level Design (HLD) and Low-Level Design (LLD), and implementing design patterns.
potential_concerns: Lack of Automotive Embedded Domain Experience:** The most critical gap is the complete absence of experience in the automotive industry, specifically automotive embedded software development. This is a mandatory requirement (5+ years).
Missing Core Automotive Technologies:** No mention of AUTOSAR architecture, Infineon Aurix microcontrollers, or CAN/CAN-FD protocols, all of which are mandatory.
Absence of Functional Safety & Process Standards:** No experience or knowledge of ISO 26262 functional safety or Automotive SPICE processes, which are mandatory for the role.
No Specific Automotive Tools/Certifications:** Lacks experience with Vector CANoe/CANalyzer, specific requirements engineering tools (DOORS, etc.), or ISO/SAE 21434 cybersecurity certification.

detailed_analysis:
SECTION 1 – Overall Assessment:
The candidate is a highly experienced Software Engineer with over 8 years of experience, primarily focused on C++ development for system software, medical devices, and gaming platforms. Their expertise spans C++ (including modern standards), Python, multithreading, HLD/LLD, and general embedded systems. They have demonstrated leadership capabilities, having led design and development efforts and managed teams.

However, this candidate is not a good fit for the Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. The job description is highly specialized, requiring deep expertise in the automotive domain, specifically with AUTOSAR architecture, ISO 26262 functional safety, Automotive SPICE processes, and specific automotive microcontrollers like Infineon Aurix. The candidate's profile lacks any mention of automotive industry experience, these critical frameworks, safety standards, or communication protocols like CAN/CAN-FD. While they possess strong general software engineering skills, the absence of domain-specific mandatory requirements makes them unsuitable for this particular role.

Recommendation: REVIEW, as the core automotive embedded requirements are not met, despite strong general software engineering experience.

SECTION 2 – Fitment Analysis Table:

CATEGORY: Total Experience
JD_REQUIRES: 5+ years of automotive embedded software development experience
CANDIDATE_HAS: Over 8 years of extensive software development experience (Staff Engineer, Software Technologist, Senior Engineer, Software Engineer roles). However, no specific mention of automotive embedded experience.
MATCH_STATUS: PARTIAL_MATCH (Meets total years, but lacks specific automotive embedded domain experience)

CATEGORY: Core Programming Languages (Embedded C/C++)
JD_REQUIRES: Expert-level proficiency in Embedded C and C++
CANDIDATE_HAS: C++ (11/14/17) and STL, Python, C language, C#.Net. Lists "Embedded Systems" as a key skill. Work experience involves "system software" and "Ultrasound Concepts" which often involve embedded aspects.
MATCH_STATUS: PARTIAL_MATCH (Strong C++ and general embedded systems knowledge, but no explicit "Embedded C" or automotive context for expert-level proficiency)

CATEGORY: Primary Domain / Industry (Automotive Embedded)
JD_REQUIRES: 5+ years of automotive embedded software development experience; Electric vehicle or hybrid powertrain experience
CANDIDATE_HAS: Experience in SanDisk (Device Design Centre - likely storage/firmware), Philips (Ultrasound Concepts - medical), L&T Technology Services (general tech services), Scientific Games (gaming), Alcatel Lucent (telecom). No mention of automotive, EV, or hybrid powertrain.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

JD_REQUIRES: Strong AUTOSAR architecture and integration experience; Designing and implementing AUTOSAR software components

CANDIDATE_HAS: Not specified.

CANDIDATE_HAS: Not specified.

CANDIDATE_HAS: "Collaborated with cross-functional teams to collect and analyze requirements" (Staff Engineer), "Gathered business and technical requirements" (Software Engineer). No specific tools mentioned.
MATCH_STATUS: PARTIAL_MATCH (Experience with requirements gathering, but no specific tools)

CATEGORY: Automotive Cybersecurity
JD_REQUIRES: ISO/SAE 21434 automotive cybersecurity certification
CANDIDATE_HAS: Not specified.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CANDIDATE_HAS: Not specified.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Tools (Vector CANoe/CANalyzer)
JD_REQUIRES: Vector CANoe, CANalyzer tools
CANDIDATE_HAS: Not specified.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Coding Standards

CANDIDATE_HAS: "Conducted code reviews to ensure adherence to coding standards". No specific mention of MISRA.
MATCH_STATUS: PARTIAL_MATCH (General adherence to coding standards, but no specific MISRA expertise)

CATEGORY: OEM Experience

CANDIDATE_HAS: SanDisk, Philips, L&T Technology Services, Scientific Games, Alcatel Lucent. None are major automotive OEMs.

CANDIDATE_HAS: Design (HLD/LLD), "Implemented the use of design patterns and best practices".
MATCH_STATUS: PARTIAL_MATCH (General HLD/LLD and design patterns, but not specific to AUTOSAR)

CANDIDATE_HAS: "Lead the design, development, and maintenance" (Staff Engineer), "Lead a team of developers, providing guidance and direction on projects" (Senior Engineer).
MATCH_STATUS: FULL_MATCH

CATEGORY: Version Control / Agile

CANDIDATE_HAS: Agile Methodology (Scrum), Git/Clear Case.
MATCH_STATUS: FULL_MATCH

SECTION 3 – Key Strengths:
- **Extensive Software Development Experience:** Over 8 years of experience in designing and developing robust software systems across various industries.
- **Strong C++ Proficiency:** Expert-level proficiency in C++ (11/14/17) and STL, along with experience in multithreading and system-level programming.
- **Leadership and Mentorship Potential:** Demonstrated experience in leading design and development efforts and leading teams of developers, providing guidance and direction.
- **Architectural Design Skills:** Experience with High-Level Design (HLD) and Low-Level Design (LLD), and implementing design patterns.

SECTION 4 – Potential Gaps / Areas to Verify:
- **Lack of Automotive Embedded Domain Experience:** The most critical gap is the complete absence of experience in the automotive industry, specifically automotive embedded software development. This is a mandatory requirement (5+ years).
- **Missing Core Automotive Technologies:** No mention of AUTOSAR architecture, Infineon Aurix microcontrollers, or CAN/CAN-FD protocols, all of which are mandatory.
- **Absence of Functional Safety & Process Standards:** No experience or knowledge of ISO 26262 functional safety or Automotive SPICE processes, which are mandatory for the role.
- **No Specific Automotive Tools/Certifications:** Lacks experience with Vector CANoe/CANalyzer, specific requirements engineering tools (DOORS, etc.), or ISO/SAE 21434 cybersecurity certification.

SECTION 5 – Experience Breakdown:
Years of Experience: 8+ years
Seniority Level: SENIOR
Core Technical Skills: C++, Python, Multithreading, NVMe, PCIe, Embedded Systems, RTOS, Data Structure and Algorithms, Design (HLD/LLD), Socket, TCP/IP, UDP.
Secondary Skills: STL, Boost, Windows Internals, Agile Methodology (Scrum), Git, Clear Case, C#, .Net, Ultrasound Concepts, Problem-solving, Adaptability, Communication.
Primary Domain: Storage/Firmware, Medical Devices, Gaming, General Software Development.
Leadership Indicators: "Lead the design, development, and maintenance of robust system software" (Staff Engineer), "Lead a team of developers, providing guidance and direction on projects" (Senior Engineer).

FINAL RECOMMENDATION:
REVIEW
Confidence: 30%
Match Score: 15%

Reason: While the candidate possesses strong general software engineering skills and leadership experience, they critically lack the mandatory automotive embedded domain experience, AUTOSAR, ISO 26262, Automotive SPICE, and specific automotive hardware/protocols required for this highly specialized Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. The absence of these core requirements makes them a poor fit, necessitating a review to determine if any transferable skills could be considered, though it's highly unlikely for such a specialized position.

llm_prompt_used:
You are a senior technical recruiter performing a detailed fitment analysis. Compare the anonymized professional profile against the job description below.

IMPORTANT RULES:
- The CV is already anonymized (all PII removed). NEVER output names, emails, phone numbers, addresses, or company locations.
- If you cannot determine something, state "Not specified" — NEVER invent details.
- Be thorough and evidence-based. Cite specific technologies, years, and project details from the CV.
- Provide a DETAILED category-by-category comparison like a professional recruitment report.
- The Overall Assessment MUST be an original analytical summary you write — do NOT copy-paste text from the CV.

OUTPUT FORMAT (FOLLOW THIS STRUCTURE EXACTLY):

SECTION 1 – Overall Assessment:
[Write a 2-3 paragraph ORIGINAL executive summary in your own words covering:
- Who the candidate is (years of experience, primary role, key expertise areas)
- Why they are / are not a good fit for this specific role
- One line recommendation
IMPORTANT: This must be YOUR analytical summary, NOT copied text from the CV. Synthesize the information into a professional recruiter assessment.]

SECTION 2 – Fitment Analysis Table:
For EACH major requirement category in the JD, provide a line in this exact format:
CATEGORY: [category name]
JD_REQUIRES: [what the JD asks for]
CANDIDATE_HAS: [what the candidate actually has, with evidence]
MATCH_STATUS: [FULL_MATCH | PARTIAL_MATCH | NO_MATCH]

(Create one entry for each of these categories, adapting to the JD:
- Total Experience
- Core Programming Languages
- Primary Domain / Industry
- Frameworks & Tools
- Architecture & Design Patterns
- Cloud / Infrastructure
- Leadership & Team Management
- Education / Certifications
- Any other JD-specific categories)

SECTION 3 – Key Strengths:
- [Strength 1 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 2 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 3 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 4 with specific evidence from CV (if applicable)]

SECTION 4 – Potential Gaps / Areas to Verify:
- [Gap 1: what is missing or unclear, and its impact]
- [Gap 2: what is missing or unclear, and its impact]
(If no gaps: "No critical gaps identified.")

SECTION 5 – Experience Breakdown:
Years of Experience: [exact number, e.g., "9 years" or "5-6 years"]
Seniority Level: [ENTRY: 0-2yrs | MID: 2-5yrs | SENIOR: 5-10yrs | LEAD: 10-15yrs | EXECUTIVE: 15+yrs]
Core Technical Skills: [List top 10 technical skills from CV]
Secondary Skills: [List additional tools, frameworks, soft skills]
Primary Domain: [Main industry/sector e.g., "Automotive Embedded", "Web Development"]
Leadership Indicators: [List concrete evidence: "Led 5-person team", "Mentored 3 juniors", or "None mentioned"]

FINAL RECOMMENDATION:
[SHORTLIST | BACKUP | REVIEW]
Confidence: [0-100]%
Match Score: [0-100]%

Reason: [2-3 sentences with specific evidence. First sentence: overall verdict with primary reason. Second: key matching evidence. Third: what tips the balance.]

===== DECISION RULES =====
- SHORTLIST: 80%+ requirements matched with strong evidence. Ready for interview.
- BACKUP: 60-79% requirements matched. Good candidate but has some gaps.
- REVIEW: <60% matched OR unclear/insufficient CV data → human must decide.
- If Confidence <70%, automatically use REVIEW regardless of match score.
- NEVER use REJECT — when in doubt, use REVIEW.

---

JOB DESCRIPTION:
Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer

MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS (Must Have):
• 5+ years of automotive embedded software development experience
• Expert-level proficiency in Embedded C and C++
• Strong AUTOSAR architecture and integration experience
• Experience with Infineon Aurix or similar automotive microcontrollers
• ISO 26262 functional safety knowledge and application
• Automotive SPICE process experience
• CAN/CAN-FD protocol expertise

NICE TO HAVE (Preferred):
• ISO/SAE 21434 automotive cybersecurity certification
• Electric vehicle or hybrid powertrain experience
• Battery management system (BMS) development
• Vector CANoe, CANalyzer tools
• Requirements engineering with DOORS, PTC Integrity, or CodeBeamer
• Experience with major OEMs (Mercedes, BMW, Volkswagen, etc.)
• Team leadership or technical architect experience
• MISRA C/C++ compliance expertise

ROLE DESCRIPTION:
We are seeking a senior embedded software engineer to lead AUTOSAR architecture design and integration for next-generation electric vehicle platforms. The ideal candidate will work with cross-functional teams to develop safety-critical automotive systems, conduct SPICE assessments, and mentor junior engineers.

Key responsibilities include:
- Designing and implementing AUTOSAR software components
- Conducting requirements engineering for electric powertrain systems
- Performing functional safety analysis per ISO 26262
- Integrating software on Infineon Aurix multicore platforms
- Leading technical reviews and SPICE assessments
- Collaborating with international OEM customers

This role requires strong technical expertise, excellent communication skills, and the ability to work independently in a fast-paced automotive development environment.
